channel.title: LSTM on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in LSTM on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: NLP (3): RNN and Sequence Modeling
item.description: Open Google Translate, swipe-type a message, or dictate a memo to your phone — all these systems consume an ordered stream of tokens and produce another. A feed-forward network processes each input independently, but language is fundamentally sequential: the meaning of “mat” in the cat sat on the mat depends on every word that came before. Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) handle this by maintaining a hidden state that evolves as they process each token. The hidden state is the network’s running summary of the past — its memory.

item.title: Time Series Forecasting (2): LSTM — Gate Mechanisms and Long-Term Dependencies
item.description:
What you will learn#

  • Why vanilla RNNs fail on long sequences and how LSTM fixes the gradient problem
  • The intuition behind each gate (forget, input, output) and the cell-state “highway”
  • How to structure inputs/outputs for one-step and multi-step time series forecasting
  • Practical recipes: regularization, sequence length, bidirectional vs stacked LSTM, when to choose LSTM vs GRU

Prerequisites#

  • Basic understanding of neural networks (forward pass, backpropagation)
  • Familiarity with PyTorch (nn.Module, tensors, optimizers)
  • Part 1 of this series (helpful but not required)

1. The Problem LSTM Solves#

A vanilla RNN updates its hidden state recursively:
